issues.description: Recursive merge accepts attacker-controlled keys and mutates shared object structures.
issues.impact: Privilege metadata can be overwritten via crafted JSON payload.

issues.description: Authorization relies on mutable global object property.
issues.impact: Unauthorized users may access admin-only endpoints.

issues.description: Nested JSON payload is processed without key allow-list validation.
issues.impact: Unexpected fields can alter risk logic and display semantics.

issues.description: Audit store keeps full request payload including sensitive policy data.
issues.impact: Operational logs may expose sensitive rule configuration.
issues.code_snippet: store.audits.push({ id: Date.now(), actor: req.user.id, payload });
issues.fixed_code_snippet: store.audits.push({ id: Date.now(), actor: req.user.id, ruleId: record.id });

issues.description: User supplied code is executed through eval.
issues.impact: Arbitrary command execution risk in application context.
issues.fixed_code_snippet: const action = allowedScripts[source]; if (!action) throw new Error('script_not_allowed'); return action(input);

issues.description: Sandbox explicitly exposes process object.
issues.impact: Sandbox code can inspect environment and runtime internals.

issues.description: Secret material is added into API response metadata.
issues.impact: API consumers can obtain secret keys and passwords.

issues.description: Execution logs persist full script, output, and secret metadata.
issues.impact: Stored logs become secondary leakage source.
issues.code_snippet: const log = { id: Date.now(), source, output, leaked };
issues.fixed_code_snippet: const log = { id: Date.now(), formId: 1, status: 'done' };

issues.description: Revoke executes asynchronously and returns success before state update.
issues.impact: Data processing may continue after user revocation request.
issues.fixed_code_snippet: const row = revoke(req.user.id); return res.json({ ok: true, row });

issues.description: Processing task reads stale consent state from local memory without confirmation.
issues.impact: Processing jobs can be queued for revoked users.

issues.description: Duplicate requests create multiple jobs for the same action.
issues.impact: Repeated submissions amplify downstream data handling.

issues.description: Consent snapshots and event store retain sensitive operational state without minimization.
issues.impact: Internal records expose privacy status changes and processing identifiers.
issues.fixed_code_snippet: events: events.map(({ id, userId }) => ({ id, userId })), snapshots: []

issues.description: Dependency source and namespace are not restricted to internal registry.
issues.impact: Malicious public package can be resolved and executed in build pipeline.

issues.description: Dependency versions use broad ranges.
issues.impact: Unexpected upstream updates may alter build behavior.

issues.description: Build artifacts are returned without checksum/signature validation.
issues.impact: Tampered artifacts may enter downstream deployment.

issues.description: Audit records include CI and npm secrets in plaintext.
issues.impact: Credential theft can compromise package publication and CI environment.

issues.description: Export endpoint reads all customer rows across tenants.
issues.impact: Cross-tenant personal and contract data disclosure.

issues.description: Object lookup ignores tenant scope.
issues.impact: Users can access records from other tenants by ID.

issues.description: No role or tenant permission check before export.
issues.impact: Any authenticated user can trigger broad data exports.

issues.description: Customer phone and contracts are stored and exported in plaintext.
issues.impact: Data theft impact is amplified if exports are leaked.

issues.category: Business Logic Bypass in Approval Workflow
issues.description: Disbursement does not verify approved state before release.
issues.impact: Funds can be released by skipping mandatory workflow stages.

issues.description: No signed transition token is validated between workflow steps.
issues.impact: State tampering is possible through direct endpoint calls.

issues.description: Single role can both approve and disburse.
issues.impact: Separation-of-duty control is absent.
issues.fixed_code_snippet: const canApprove = req.user.role === 'reviewer'; const canDisburse = req.user.role === 'disburser';

issues.description: Borrower ID card data is kept in plaintext records and logs.
issues.impact: PII disclosure risk in storage and operational dumps.

issues.category: GraphQL Authorization Bypass via Nested Query
issues.description: Nested field authorization always returns true.
issues.impact: Users can query data outside their permitted scope.

issues.description: Query resolver returns full user objects with sensitive fields.
issues.impact: Single request discloses excessive personal information.

issues.description: Introspection query is enabled in production endpoint.
issues.impact: Attackers can enumerate schema and target sensitive fields.

issues.description: Sensitive fields are persisted in plaintext and returned through views.
issues.impact: Data breach impact increases due to direct readability.
issues.fixed_code_snippet: return store.users.map(({ id, name, permission_scope }) => ({ id, name, permission_scope }));

issues.description: Login query merges attacker-supplied operators into auth condition.
issues.impact: Authentication bypass via $ne/$regex payloads.
issues.fixed_code_snippet: const query = { username: String(req.body.username), password_hash: String(req.body.password_hash) };

issues.description: Operator keys are interpreted directly from input objects.
issues.impact: Query semantics can be altered to broaden match scope.
issues.fixed_code_snippet: if (typeof filter[k] === 'object') { throw new Error('invalid filter'); }

issues.description: Different response status and message reveal whether user exists.
issues.impact: Attackers can enumerate valid usernames.

issues.description: Password hashes, reset tokens and phone numbers are directly exposed in API and views.
issues.impact: Compromised responses aid account takeover and privacy breach.
issues.fixed_code_snippet: res.json({ ok: true, user: { id: user.id, username: user.username, nickname_style: user.nickname_style } });

issues.description: Replay protection window and unique event checks are missing.
issues.impact: Same callback can be replayed to create repeated ledger entries.
issues.fixed_code_snippet: if (isReplay(p.payment_id, p.callback_timestamp)) return res.status(409).json({ ok: false });

issues.description: Signature check validates only static prefix.
issues.impact: Forged callbacks may pass validation.

issues.description: Nonce store exists but is never used for deduplication.
issues.impact: No replay nonce tracking enables repeated acceptance.

issues.description: Payment metadata with signature is retained in plaintext logs.
issues.impact: Sensitive callback data leakage can aid signature abuse.
issues.fixed_code_snippet: store.payments.push({ payment_id: p.payment_id, amount: p.amount, signature_hash: sha256(p.signature) });

issues.description: Master key is hardcoded and rotate operation reuses same key material.
issues.impact: Historical data remains decryptable after supposed key rotation.

issues.description: Fixed IV is reused for all encryption operations.
issues.impact: Ciphertext patterns become predictable and analyzable.

issues.description: Backups contain plaintext legacy key values.
issues.impact: Backup leakage enables decryption of archived sensitive data.

issues.description: Backup endpoint returns raw backup content without redaction.
issues.impact: Sensitive cryptographic metadata can be exfiltrated.
issues.fixed_code_snippet: res.json({ ok: true, backup: store.backups.map(({ id, key_ref }) => ({ id, key_ref })) });
